name: Lesotho
avoid: November to March brings storms, muddy highland roads, and frequent cloud cover around Semonkong and Sani Pass
Do not attempt Sani Pass or the rougher highland legs in a normal sedan; a true 4x4 and rental border letter are essential
Night driving is a bad idea because of livestock, potholes, and very limited lighting outside Maseru
stopoverNote: Most India itineraries work best via Johannesburg, then onward to Maseru by short flight or road. Minibus taxis cover Maseru, Semonkong, and Butha-Buthe on a budget, but a 4x4 is the practical default once you commit to Ts'ehlanyane and Sani Pass.

cities.name: Maseru
cities.notes: Capital base for currency, SIM, 4x4 pickup, Basotho craft stops, and the easiest start before the mountain legs

cities.name: Semonkong
cities.notes: Gateway to Maletsunyane Falls, Basotho pony trekking, and the rough-road heart of Lesotho's highlands

cities.name: Ts'ehlanyane National Park
cities.notes: Northern mountain forest reserve anchored by Maliba Lodge, river trails, and easy access toward Leribe's dinosaur sites

cities.name: Sani Pass
cities.notes: The classic escarpment crossing for 4x4 travelers, with Sani Top lodges, border formalities, and huge Drakensberg views

itinerary.sim: Vodacom Lesotho is the safest first pick around Maseru, with Econet Telecom Lesotho as the backup. Budget about ₹460–₹920 for enough data to handle offline maps, lodge messaging, and weather checks before the pass.

days.theme: Arrival logistics, Basotho food, and setting up the mountain loop properly

activities.name: Pick up a true 4x4 from Lesane Elites Car Rentals or Avis Maseru before heading into the highlands
Browse the Basotho Hat craft market and central Maseru for blankets, woven souvenirs, and cash supplies
Our Lady of Victories Cathedral and easy city orientation walk
Optional Thaba-Bosiu Cultural Village outing if you land early enough for one major historical stop

days.theme: Optional buffer day for history, shopping, and an easier start before the rougher roads

activities.name: Thaba-Bosiu Cultural Village museum and plateau viewpoint revisit
Pioneer Mall and local cafe reset before the drive south or north
Retain the 4x4 for the following Semonkong departure day

days.theme: Get into the mountains, reach the lodge, and see Maletsunyane Falls before sunset

activities.name: Minibus taxi from Maseru main rank to Semonkong if you are staying budget and traveling light
4x4 self-drive or arranged transfer on the rough highland road if you want the practical option
Maletsunyane Falls community viewpoint fee
Semonkong village walk and sunset over the gorge

days.theme: Classic Basotho pony trekking day to the falls and the shepherd trails around the plateau

activities.name: Semonkong Lodge 1-hour Basotho pony outride
Semonkong Lodge half-day pony trek to Maletsunyane Falls
Semonkong Lodge full-day pony trek if you want the longer mountain version
Optional second stop at the Maletsunyane viewpoints for photos in different light

days.theme: Weather buffer or slow highlands day for hiking, village time, and road recovery

activities.name: Easy guided village walk or informal local guide tip around Semonkong
Repeat a short pony ride if cloud or rain spoiled the previous day
Retain the 4x4 for the long transfer north toward Ts'ehlanyane

days.theme: Drive north into the forest reserve and settle into the easier, greener side of Lesotho

activities.name: 4x4 drive from Semonkong to the Ts'ehlanyane/Maliba area with fuel, snack, and road-condition buffer
Self-guided river trail or lower forest walk inside Ts'ehlanyane National Park
Optional Maliba-arranged guided hike if you want a ranger or local guide rather than doing the shorter trails yourself

days.theme: Mix a forest morning with Lesotho's famous dinosaur footprint stop in nearby Leribe district

activities.name: Subeng Dinosaur Footprints community guide fee
Short return drive from the park or Butha-Buthe area to the Subeng site
Back-in-park waterfall or forest trail for the late afternoon

days.theme: Climb to Sani Top, handle the border costs, and enjoy the signature escarpment views

activities.name: Retain a proper 4x4 for the Sani Pass approach and the high-altitude lodge access
Lesotho tourism levy for foreign visitors at Sani Pass border control
Foreign-registered vehicle road or toll fee at the border
Stop at Sani Mountain Lodge's Highest Pub in Africa and the nearby escarpment viewpoints

days.theme: Optional extra day for weather margin, sunrise photos, or a slow exit to South Africa or back to Maseru

activities.name: Sunrise photography or a short escarpment walk from the lodge area
4x4 drive back to Maseru or descent toward Underberg with fuel buffer

connections.method: Minibus taxis run from Maseru on the budget route, but a 4x4 or arranged transfer is far easier once you factor luggage and the rougher final stretch
Public transport means changing vehicles via Maseru, so most travelers treat this as a full self-drive or private-transfer day
This is the leg where the retained 4x4 really earns its keep; weather and road conditions make piecemeal minibus connections impractical
Return by 4x4 to Maseru, or descend toward Underberg if you are exiting into South Africa rather than closing the loop in Lesotho

extras: May to September is the sweet spot for this exact loop: roads are drier, pony trails are easier to enjoy, and Sani Pass visibility is far better than in the summer storm season.
Carry small cash for the Maletsunyane community viewpoint, the Sani tourism levy, and the Subeng dinosaur-footprints guide fee because card facilities can be unreliable away from Maseru.
Minibus taxis are real and useful in Lesotho, especially from Maseru to Semonkong or Butha-Buthe, but they do not solve last-mile transfers to lodges or the Sani Pass highlands segment.
If your rental car is South African, confirm the cross-border letter and whether the quoted rate already includes Lesotho paperwork before you leave the desk.
Sani Pass is not a sedan road. Even experienced self-drivers should treat a proper low-range 4x4 as essential, not optional.
